subject: Upgrade dependency versions for security
commit_message:
Upgrade dependency versions for security

This change upgrades dependencies to versions that ve no or lesser
security issues than the current versions.

Issue-ID: POLICY-1644
Change-Id: Ib6dc8193c8da2547046791d82956019e0c741544
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-apex-pdp-master-verify-java/868/

Patch Set 1:

I didn't know that if you change the version of the dependency in the dependencymanagement secction of your main POM, it changes the version of that dependency on all inclusions of that dependency in all POMs below that master POM, at least that's how it looks in Eclipse.

Let's see what the CLM says about this.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-apex-pdp-master-verify-java/868/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-apex-pdp-master-verify-java/868

Patch Set 1:

oparent is declaring it - you are overriding here.

Patch Set 1:

I'm overriding jackson-core, oparent doesn't specify jackson-databind.

Patch Set 1:

> I didn't know that if you change the version of the dependency in
 > the dependencymanagement secction of your main POM, it changes the
 > version of that dependency on all inclusions of that dependency in
 > all POMs below that master POM, at least that's how it looks in
 > Eclipse.
 > 
 > Let's see what the CLM says about this.

Yes, this is true for dependency management. If you change the version then the new version takes effect for all child unless they have specified the version in child too.

Patch Set 1:

Can you have a look at this please?

Patch Set 1:

> Can you have a look at this please?

I'll take a look as soon as I finish investigating the merge issue with policy/api.

Patch Set 1:

> > Can you have a look at this please?
 > 
 > I'll take a look as soon as I finish investigating the merge issue
 > with policy/api.

FYI: I changed the version in my local copy of oparent to the version specified in this review.  Rebuilding/installing all of my local repos to ensure nothing breaks.  However, I don't really expect any breakage, as the other policy repos explicitly exclude jackson-databind.  Nevertheless, we'll see - I know drools-pdp has some junit tests to ensure that the gson-encoded messages match the jackson-encoded messages, so the junit tests there must implicitly include jackson-databind.

Patch Set 1:

When I tried this on policy-common, it broke some unit tests in endpoints.

Patch Set 1:

> When I tried this on policy-common, it broke some unit tests in
 > endpoints.

Policy/common built fine when I changed it in oparent.  Models just succeed, too.  I'll try drools-pdp next and, if that works, I'll back the change out of oparent and put it directly into policy/common - to mirror your test (hopefully).

Patch Set 1:

Maybe we should put it in policy parent rather than in policy-common, then we wouldn't need this review either.

Patch Set 1:

> Maybe we should put it in policy parent rather than in
 > policy-common, then we wouldn't need this review either.

If we put it in parent, will it override the one in oparent for ALL of the policy repos?  If so, then that would be fine with me.  (Or perhaps it should just be changed in oparent - if that's a viable option.)

Patch Set 1:

I think putting it in our parent should overide it in all our repos, except Engine where I think they use a specific version.

Patch Set 1:

> I think putting it in our parent should overide it in all our
 > repos, except Engine where I think they use a specific version.

I have to re-run my tests - forgot that all of the version numbers have to change to snapshot to pick up the change in dependent repos.

Patch Set 1:

> I think putting it in our parent should overide it in all our
 > repos, except Engine where I think they use a specific version.

Yes, we should probably abandon this review and put it in parent.  I'll go ahead and do the latter since I've already spent the time identifying artifacts.  The version will need to be removed from the other repos that specify it - maybe I'll leave that up to you to do once the change to parent has been merged.
Uploaded patch set 2.
